name: Coordinated Inauthentic Behaviour
description: Detection, conceptualisation and longitudinal study of coordinated link sharing and inauthentic networks on social platforms, including the CooRnet methodological lineage and its application to elections and problematic content.

name: Information Disorder and Disinformation
description: Empirical and conceptual work on misinformation, disinformation and 'fake news' within hybrid media systems, including problematic health content and narrative monitoring across European contexts.

name: Platform Governance, APIs and Data Access
description: Studies of platform policy changes (e.g. Meta's political content reduction, CrowdTangle shutdown), the DSA-era research API landscape, and the methodological consequences of walled-garden access for social media research.

name: Elections and Political Communication
description: Mapping of news coverage, partisan attention and platform dynamics around Italian, German, Brazilian and European elections — the core empirical terrain of the MINE programme.

name: MINE — Mapping Italian News
description: The institutional and intellectual umbrella for the PI's research line since 2017, encompassing successive sub-projects on Italian media, elections and social platforms.

name: Computational Methods and LLMs for Communication Research
description: Development and validation of computational pipelines — embeddings, LLM-in-the-loop classification, network analysis — for political discourse and news studies, addressing reliability and reproducibility.

name: Generative AI and Media Manipulation
description: Emerging line on generative-AI-enabled persuasion, synthetic media in coordinated promotion (e.g. gambling), and AI-assisted verification tools for journalists and fact-checkers.

name: News Ecosystems, Partisanship and Polarisation
description: Analyses of partisan attention scores, hyperpartisan networks, alternative influence networks and audience insularity around news on Facebook and other platforms.

name: Health Misinformation and COVID-19
description: Research on problematic health content circulation, COVID-19 fact-checking infrastructures, and coordinated networks around health information in Italy, India and Nigeria.

name: Internet Studies and Sociocybernetics
description: Foundational and theoretical reflections bridging social systems theory, sociocybernetics and internet studies, including the epistemology of digital social data.

name: Social TV, Second Screen and Networked Audiences
description: Long-running line on TV audience participation, second-screen engagement and networked intimacy on Twitter and Facebook, including the seminal Second Screen and Participation study.

name: Digital Methods and Research Ethics
description: Methodological contributions on using Facebook, Twitter and YouTube as research data sources, Facebook Ads for survey recruitment, and the ethics of computational social science.

name: Online Radicalization, Extremism, and the Far Right on Social Media
description: Empirical and review studies examining how extremist and radicalizing content is produced, circulated, and moderated across social media platforms, including jihadist evasion of TikTok moderation, far-right affective normalization on Instagram, and a 25-year scoping review of online radicalization research.

name: Climate Communication, Visualization, and Misinformation
description: Studies investigating how climate change is communicated, visualized, denied, or contested across media, including systematic reviews of climate visualization, experimental tests of climate misinformation, megastudies of persuasive climate messages, and visual-first analysis of climate denial on Instagram.

name: Platform Affordances and the Transformation of Social Media Publics
description: Reflective and empirical essays examining how platform architectures, affordances, and political economies are reshaping online publics—covering TikTok's algorithmic clustering, the decade-long trajectory of platformization, scam-saturated platform economies, and nostalgic critiques of the old internet.

name: Sociotechnical AI Evaluation, Red-Teaming, and Public Participation
description: Essays and perspectives framing AI safety, evaluation, and accountability as inherently sociotechnical problems requiring labor analysis, public involvement, and critical attention to political economy rather than purely technical fixes.

name: Polarization, Depolarization, and Deliberative Online Discourse
description: Studies addressing polarization in online and political contexts, including systematic review of depolarization recommendations, theoretical proposals for deliberative reciprocity, and experimental evaluation of balanced news chatbots for users with conspiratorial beliefs.
